what_it_does: This file stores YAML frontmatter metadata for an image attachment that references a media asset in the design-source channel memory.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: References a media asset via a custom image:// URI scheme
Records an exact ISO 8601 addition timestamp
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Follows an att-* id naming convention
Uses a channel-qualified URI for the attachment target
concerns: The target identifier p-03 is opaque without additional context
No title, alt text, MIME type, or checksum is recorded
No author or source is attached to the timestamp
notes: A minimal metadata stub that links an image asset to a design-source channel memory entry.
questions: What does image p-03 actually depict?
Which specific design-source memory entry does this attachment belong to?
Is the referenced image file present and versioned elsewhere?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is labeled and structured as an attachment record, and it performs exactly that function by storing id, type, target, and timestamp.
intention_signals: It appears designed to register an image asset as a channel-memory attachment so media can be referenced by URI without being embedded in the ledger file itself.
novelty: It uses a custom image:// URI scheme to point to channel-local media rather than a conventional relative file path.
contribution: It contributes a precise, timestamped attachment record that preserves the link between a design-source channel memory and a specific image asset location.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML attachment metadata record for an image named p-03 in the design-source channel, added on 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to register an image attachment so channel memory can reference the company-info media asset p-03.
